skeptical_weaknesses.critique: Employer-side review would argue that the chronology still contains material unexplained gaps around GAP-001.

repair_guidance.cautious_rewrite: On the current record, chronology gap remains chronology-sensitive and should stay provisional until the missing sequence is documented.
repair_guidance.evidence_that_would_repair: Dated documents that bridge the missing period, especially records tied to the same issue track.
repair_guidance.how_to_fix: Fill the gap with dated documents, meeting records, or contemporaneous correspondence.

skeptical_weaknesses.title: chronology_problem

skeptical_weaknesses.critique: Employer-side review would foreground this competing explanation: Manager may have misunderstood the process order.

repair_guidance.cautious_rewrite: Competing explanation can currently be read in more than one way, so the safer formulation is that the record raises a concern pattern rather than proving a one-sided explanation.
repair_guidance.evidence_that_would_repair: Records that distinguish the claimant's sequence from ordinary workflow or operational conditions.
repair_guidance.how_to_fix: Show why the alternative explanation does not fit the timing, document trail, or treatment pattern.

repair_guidance.evidence_that_would_repair: Comparator examples from the same process step or decision-maker
repair_guidance.how_to_fix: Request the missing document directly and tie it to the relevant issue track or chronology gap.

skeptical_weaknesses.title: overstated_comparison

skeptical_weaknesses.critique: Employer-side review would argue that hierarchy, gatekeeping, and power analysis remain under-documented because no org context was supplied.

repair_guidance.cautious_rewrite: Org context should be framed as incomplete until the underlying documentary support is obtained.
